web.title: GitHub - antoinezambelli/forge: A Python framework for self-hosted ...
web.description: A reliability layer for self-hosted LLM tool-calling. You give forge a set of tools; the model calls whichever it wants in whatever order.

Upload coverage.xml to codecov from Python 3.13 matrix run - Add codecov badge to README Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) ") | 3 months agoApr 2, 2026 | | [docs](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/tree/main/docs "docs") | [docs](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/tree/main/docs "docs") | [v0.7.4: malformed-args → tool-error channel + 32GB eval tier (](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/commit/bd99f4df0a7aab2fd4db2e6dae7f810a32617d76 "v0.7.4: malformed-args → tool-error channel + 32GB eval tier (#102) * Args-validation: route malformed args through tool-error channel Models occasionally emit a structurally valid tool call with malformed args content (e.g. arguments=\"\" instead of arguments=\"{}\"). Pydantic rejected at ToolCall construction, crashing the stage with ValidationError. Observed at 86% of error rows on Qwen3-Next prompt mode (77/89), same family on Qwen3.6 (rig-02). This is conceptually \"tool called with bad args\" — the call exists, the inputs are wrong — same as FileNotFoundError at runtime. Should ride the tool-error channel with max_tool_errors=2 budget, not crash. - ToolCall / TextResponse: BaseModel → @dataclass. args is no longer validated at construction; ResponseValidator enforces dict-shape. Audit: no .model_* API on ToolCall anywhere in forge. - ResponseValidator: new args-shape branch after unknown-tool check. Unknown-tool runs first (cheap; no point validating args on a hallucinated tool name). - nudges.tool_arg_validation_nudge: schema-derived message naming the tool, the received args type, and the required JSON-object shape. - inference: parse-error nudges drain max_tool_errors (record_result) not max_retries (record_retry). Message prefix [ToolArgValidationError] vs [UnknownTool]. - Exhaustion message simplified: includes which budget and nudge kind. Smoke (Ministral-3-14B-Reasoning, 26 scenarios × 25 runs prompt-mode): score 78.77% (vs v0.7.0 baseline 79.5% at n=50). Delta within ±1.6% noise band — no regression. Patch never tripped on this model; this is a regression check, full bake on a model that hits the path to follow. 884 tests pass. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * batch_eval/sampling: 32GB-eval lineup — Qwen3.6-27B, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD, Nemotron-3-Nano GGUF + server-flag + sampling-default entries for the three rig-02 32GB-tier models added for the v0.7.1 run. Launch config of record for eval_results_rig-02_v0.7.1.jsonl (31,200 rows). * clients: route malformed tool-call args through the tool-error channel Unify all OpenAI-shape clients on one decode_tool_args helper (clients/base.py): JSON-string args are parsed; malformed or non-dict payloads ride through on the ToolCall as raw (non-dict) args instead of collapsing to a TextResponse (openai_compat, vllm, llamafile) or raising (anthropic streaming). ResponseValidator's args-shape check then routes them to the tool-error channel + max_tool_errors budget — the same lane as a runtime tool error — rather than a retry nudge. Completes the client normalization #86 started (one decoder, all clients) and keeps fail-loud (never coerced to {}). Also closes an unguarded json.loads crash in the anthropic streaming finalize. Behavior change: structural malformed-args now drains max_tool_errors (2), not max_retries (3), in proxy mode. Wire-invisible; no public signature changes. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * review fixes: honest ToolCall.args type + de-stale comments + llamafile malformed test From the helper-vs-inline structural review (verdict: keep the shared decode_tool_args helper). Two correctness/honesty caveats actioned: - ToolCall.args annotated dict[str, Any] while the runtime contract now intentionally allows non-dicts (the docstring already says so). Widen to Any so the type stops lying. - Stale comments in openai_compat/vllm streaming finalize still claimed malformed args yield a retry-driving TextResponse; corrected to the raw-args → tool-error-channel routing (they invited the exact drift the helper prevents). - Add an explicit llamafile malformed-args test (non-stream native path). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * guardrails: make tool-call faults consistent across all three modes + expose proxy max_tool_errors From the H1/H2 design review. Two consistency gaps closed: 1. The Guardrails middleware facade recorded EVERY validator failure as a retry (max_retries) and returned action='retry' — diverging from run_inference/proxy, where malformed args drain the tool-error budget and tool-call faults ride the tool channel. The facade now: - routes malformed args (tool_arg_validation) to max_tool_errors, - returns a new action='tool_error' for tool-call faults (unknown tool name OR malformed args), with nudge.role='tool' so callers emit the correction on the tool-result channel. Channel vs budget are now two explicit kind-sets in nudge.py (TOOL_CHANNEL_KINDS ⊃ TOOL_ERROR_KINDS); unknown-tool rides the tool channel but still drains the retry budget, matching run_inference. _TOOL_ERROR_KINDS moved from inference.py to the shared nudge module. 2. Proxy exposed max_retries but not max_tool_errors, hiding the budget exactly where malformed-arg recovery now matters. Added --max-tool-errors (default 2) threaded ProxyServer → HTTPServer → handler → ErrorTracker. Nobody depends on the middleware facade yet, so the CheckResult.action addition is free. Channel parity in run_inference unchanged (it emits role=tool for list-branch corrections regardless of nudge.role). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * steps: guard arg-match prereqs against non-dict args + ADR-016 Closes the last crash vector from the design review (hole 4): StepTracker.check_prerequisites did args.get(match_arg), which raises on a non-dict args. ResponseValidator fences this before dispatch in the runner/proxy, but a granular caller that bypasses check() could reach it directly. Treat non-dict args as unsatisfied (block, don't crash). ADR-016 records the malformed-args → tool-error-channel decision in its honest framing: a native-mode conditioning bet, not an ontology claim; prompt mode degrades to the prior retry shape; the tool-error budget coupling is deliberate but revisitable. CHANGELOG held for release time. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval data: tag generations + add 32GB tier (v0.7.4) Inject a per-row `gen` field so one dashboard can fold eval waves run against different code states. gen is a comparability epoch, not a release version: v0.6.0 -> gen 1 (carries the Anthropic ablation + Retired-tier models, neither re-run since), and v0.7.0 plus the new 32GB tier -> gen 2. Rename the 32GB wave to eval_results_v0.7.4.jsonl (its landing release) and keep it as a separate file beside v0.7.0 — same gen, distinct wave, so each wave keeps its own landing commit for reproducibility. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval dashboard: eval-generation badges + retired toggle report.py now accepts multiple result files and keeps the newest gen per config (dedup_latest_gen), so the board folds all generations into one view. Lagging rows (gen < newest) get a superscript badge backed by a commit/date legend; Retired-tier models are carried forward but hidden by default (--include-retired, or a sidebar checkbox in the HTML). Adds MODEL_FAMILIES entries for the 6 32GB models so they render clean family names and cross-backend keys instead of raw GGUF stems. React dashboard: Show-retired checkbox (dimmed rows + a \"retired\" pill), superscript gen badges with provenance tooltips from the data blob. Regenerated docs/results/ from the three gen-tagged datasets. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * release: 0.7.4 — version bump, CHANGELOG, registry 32GB tier Bump 0.7.3 -> 0.7.4 and add the 0.7.4 CHANGELOG entry (malformed args -> tool-error channel; 32GB eval tier + dashboard eval-generations). Move the 6 32GB models (Mistral-Small-3.2, Qwen3.5/3.6 27-35B, Nemotron-3 Nano) from Unpublished to Current now that they're in the published eval, and reword the tier definitions for the dashboard's eval generations. Also scrub a stale bring-up note from the Qwen3.5-122B footnote (it leaked operator smoke-probe process into a public doc) and exclude the built dashboard dist/ from the hatchling sdist sweep. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * changelog: de-escalate dataclass entry from BREAKING to Changed The ToolCall/TextResponse pydantic->dataclass move only breaks callers who serialized these via the pydantic .model_* API or relied on construction-time validation; attribute reads and keyword construction are unchanged. Reserving BREAKING for forced-migration changes (cf. 0.7.3 --mode rename) keeps the badge meaningful. --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) ") [#102](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/pull/102) [)](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/commit/bd99f4df0a7aab2fd4db2e6dae7f810a32617d76 "v0.7.4: malformed-args → tool-error channel + 32GB eval tier (#102) * Args-validation: route malformed args through tool-error channel Models occasionally emit a structurally valid tool call with malformed args content (e.g. arguments=\"\" instead of arguments=\"{}\"). Reserving BREAKING for forced-migration changes (cf. 0.7.3 --mode rename) keeps the badge meaningful. --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) ") | 2 days agoJun 3, 2026 | | [examples](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/tree/main/examples "examples") | [examples](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/tree/main/examples "examples") | [Document middleware caller responsibilities in User Guide and foreign…](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/commit/8b8b54da7703f2874379e26b7923eabe06e8dc27 "Document middleware caller responsibilities in User Guide and foreign_loop example (#46) Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) ") | 3 months agoApr 2, 2026 | | [scripts](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/tree/main/scripts "scripts") | [scripts](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/tree/main/scripts "scripts") | [refactor(clients): disambiguate model identity + fix /v1/models stub (](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/commit/ad1628022da9a27f0ad055c984231a203d338f87 "refactor(clients): disambiguate model identity + fix /v1/models stub (#100) (#101) * refactor(clients): disambiguate model identity into model + sampling_key Every client now uses two unambiguously-named identity attributes: - self.model the wire \"model\" field, sent verbatim to the backend - self.sampling_key the registry-lookup key for apply_sampling_defaults Previously self.model meant different things across clients: a derived registry-lookup stem on VLLMClient, but the wire id (doubling as the key) on ollama/openai_compat/anthropic/llamafile. That overload was the smell. VLLMClient is the only client that changes meaningfully: its wire id moves from self.model_path to self.model (the value sent is byte-identical), and the derived stem moves from self.model to self.sampling_key. The other four clients keep self.model exactly as-is and gain a self.sampling_key alias so the registry lookup reads an unambiguous name. self.model_path is dropped as an attribute (nothing external read it). The --model-path CLI flag and VLLMClient(model_path=...) ctor param are unchanged model_path lives on only at the locked boundary. Zero proxy-user impact: CLI flags, ctor kwargs, wire values, and all output schemas (/v1/models, completion model echo, eval JSONL) are untouched. Internal-only; no version bump (rides the next release). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * fix(proxy): report real model from /v1/models + add model to LLMClient protocol Closes #100. /v1/models previously returned a hardcoded {\"id\": \"forge\"} stub regardless of the backend. It now reports self._client.model — the real wire id every client carries after the identity refactor (served-model-name for vLLM, gguf stem for llama.cpp, model tag for ollama). No fallback: a client lacking .model raises rather than serving a lie. Elevates model: str to the LLMClient protocol (sibling of api_format), making the wire-id attribute a real contract now that all five clients set it uniformly. No type-checker runs in CI today, so this is a documented contract rather than an enforced one — the direct read is what does the work. Also extends scripts/smoke_test_proxy.py with /v1/models coverage (absent before): the external \"default\" placeholder case and the configured-model case. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 ") [#…](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/issues/100) | 3 days agoJun 1, 2026 | | [src/forge](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/tree/main/src/forge "This path skips through empty directories") | [src/forge](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/tree/main/src/forge "This path skips through empty directories") | [v0.7.4: malformed-args → tool-error channel + 32GB eval tier (](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/commit/bd99f4df0a7aab2fd4db2e6dae7f810a32617d76 "v0.7.4: malformed-args → tool-error channel + 32GB eval tier (#102) * Args-validation: route malformed args through tool-error channel Models occasionally emit a structurally valid tool call with malformed args content (e.g. arguments=\"\" instead of arguments=\"{}\"). Pydantic rejected at ToolCall construction, crashing the stage with ValidationError. Observed at 86% of error rows on Qwen3-Next prompt mode (77/89), same family on Qwen3.6 (rig-02). This is conceptually \"tool called with bad args\" — the call exists, the inputs are wrong — same as FileNotFoundError at runtime. Should ride the tool-error channel with max_tool_errors=2 budget, not crash. - ToolCall / TextResponse: BaseModel → @dataclass. args is no longer validated at construction; ResponseValidator enforces dict-shape. Audit: no .model_* API on ToolCall anywhere in forge. - ResponseValidator: new args-shape branch after unknown-tool check. Unknown-tool runs first (cheap; no point validating args on a hallucinated tool name). - nudges.tool_arg_validation_nudge: schema-derived message naming the tool, the received args type, and the required JSON-object shape. - inference: parse-error nudges drain max_tool_errors (record_result) not max_retries (record_retry). Message prefix [ToolArgValidationError] vs [UnknownTool]. - Exhaustion message simplified: includes which budget and nudge kind. Smoke (Ministral-3-14B-Reasoning, 26 scenarios × 25 runs prompt-mode): score 78.77% (vs v0.7.0 baseline 79.5% at n=50). Delta within ±1.6% noise band — no regression. Patch never tripped on this model; this is a regression check, full bake on a model that hits the path to follow. 884 tests pass. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * batch_eval/sampling: 32GB-eval lineup — Qwen3.6-27B, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD, Nemotron-3-Nano GGUF + server-flag + sampling-default entries for the three rig-02 32GB-tier models added for the v0.7.1 run. Launch config of record for eval_results_rig-02_v0.7.1.jsonl (31,200 rows). * clients: route malformed tool-call args through the tool-error channel Unify all OpenAI-shape clients on one decode_tool_args helper (clients/base.py): JSON-string args are parsed; malformed or non-dict payloads ride through on the ToolCall as raw (non-dict) args instead of collapsing to a TextResponse (openai_compat, vllm, llamafile) or raising (anthropic streaming). ResponseValidator's args-shape check then routes them to the tool-error channel + max_tool_errors budget — the same lane as a runtime tool error — rather than a retry nudge. Completes the client normalization #86 started (one decoder, all clients) and keeps fail-loud (never coerced to {}). Also closes an unguarded json.loads crash in the anthropic streaming finalize. Behavior change: structural malformed-args now drains max_tool_errors (2), not max_retries (3), in proxy mode. Wire-invisible; no public signature changes. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * review fixes: honest ToolCall.args type + de-stale comments + llamafile malformed test From the helper-vs-inline structural review (verdict: keep the shared decode_tool_args helper). Two correctness/honesty caveats actioned: - ToolCall.args annotated dict[str, Any] while the runtime contract now intentionally allows non-dicts (the docstring already says so). Widen to Any so the type stops lying. - Stale comments in openai_compat/vllm streaming finalize still claimed malformed args yield a retry-driving TextResponse; corrected to the raw-args → tool-error-channel routing (they invited the exact drift the helper prevents). - Add an explicit llamafile malformed-args test (non-stream native path). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * guardrails: make tool-call faults consistent across all three modes + expose proxy max_tool_errors From the H1/H2 design review. Two consistency gaps closed: 1. The Guardrails middleware facade recorded EVERY validator failure as a retry (max_retries) and returned action='retry' — diverging from run_inference/proxy, where malformed args drain the tool-error budget and tool-call faults ride the tool channel. The facade now: - routes malformed args (tool_arg_validation) to max_tool_errors, - returns a new action='tool_error' for tool-call faults (unknown tool name OR malformed args), with nudge.role='tool' so callers emit the correction on the tool-result channel. Channel vs budget are now two explicit kind-sets in nudge.py (TOOL_CHANNEL_KINDS ⊃ TOOL_ERROR_KINDS); unknown-tool rides the tool channel but still drains the retry budget, matching run_inference. _TOOL_ERROR_KINDS moved from inference.py to the shared nudge module. 2. Proxy exposed max_retries but not max_tool_errors, hiding the budget exactly where malformed-arg recovery now matters. Added --max-tool-errors (default 2) threaded ProxyServer → HTTPServer → handler → ErrorTracker. Nobody depends on the middleware facade yet, so the CheckResult.action addition is free. Channel parity in run_inference unchanged (it emits role=tool for list-branch corrections regardless of nudge.role). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * steps: guard arg-match prereqs against non-dict args + ADR-016 Closes the last crash vector from the design review (hole 4): StepTracker.check_prerequisites did args.get(match_arg), which raises on a non-dict args. ResponseValidator fences this before dispatch in the runner/proxy, but a granular caller that bypasses check() could reach it directly. Treat non-dict args as unsatisfied (block, don't crash). ADR-016 records the malformed-args → tool-error-channel decision in its honest framing: a native-mode conditioning bet, not an ontology claim; prompt mode degrades to the prior retry shape; the tool-error budget coupling is deliberate but revisitable. CHANGELOG held for release time. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval data: tag generations + add 32GB tier (v0.7.4) Inject a per-row `gen` field so one dashboard can fold eval waves run against different code states. gen is a comparability epoch, not a release version: v0.6.0 -> gen 1 (carries the Anthropic ablation + Retired-tier models, neither re-run since), and v0.7.0 plus the new 32GB tier -> gen 2. Rename the 32GB wave to eval_results_v0.7.4.jsonl (its landing release) and keep it as a separate file beside v0.7.0 — same gen, distinct wave, so each wave keeps its own landing commit for reproducibility. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval dashboard: eval-generation badges + retired toggle report.py now accepts multiple result files and keeps the newest gen per config (dedup_latest_gen), so the board folds all generations into one view. Lagging rows (gen < newest) get a superscript badge backed by a commit/date legend; Retired-tier models are carried forward but hidden by default (--include-retired, or a sidebar checkbox in the HTML). Adds MODEL_FAMILIES entries for the 6 32GB models so they render clean family names and cross-backend keys instead of raw GGUF stems. React dashboard: Show-retired checkbox (dimmed rows + a \"retired\" pill), superscript gen badges with provenance tooltips from the data blob. Regenerated docs/results/ from the three gen-tagged datasets. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * release: 0.7.4 — version bump, CHANGELOG, registry 32GB tier Bump 0.7.3 -> 0.7.4 and add the 0.7.4 CHANGELOG entry (malformed args -> tool-error channel; 32GB eval tier + dashboard eval-generations). Move the 6 32GB models (Mistral-Small-3.2, Qwen3.5/3.6 27-35B, Nemotron-3 Nano) from Unpublished to Current now that they're in the published eval, and reword the tier definitions for the dashboard's eval generations. Also scrub a stale bring-up note from the Qwen3.5-122B footnote (it leaked operator smoke-probe process into a public doc) and exclude the built dashboard dist/ from the hatchling sdist sweep. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * changelog: de-escalate dataclass entry from BREAKING to Changed The ToolCall/TextResponse pydantic->dataclass move only breaks callers who serialized these via the pydantic .model_* API or relied on construction-time validation; attribute reads and keyword construction are unchanged. Reserving BREAKING for forced-migration changes (cf. 0.7.3 --mode rename) keeps the badge meaningful. --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) ") [#102](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/pull/102) [)](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/commit/bd99f4df0a7aab2fd4db2e6dae7f810a32617d76 "v0.7.4: malformed-args → tool-error channel + 32GB eval tier (#102) * Args-validation: route malformed args through tool-error channel Models occasionally emit a structurally valid tool call with malformed args content (e.g. arguments=\"\" instead of arguments=\"{}\"). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * guardrails: make tool-call faults consistent across all three modes + expose proxy max_tool_errors From the H1/H2 design review. Two consistency gaps closed: 1. The Guardrails middleware facade recorded EVERY validator failure as a retry (max_retries) and returned action='retry' — diverging from run_inference/proxy, where malformed args drain the tool-error budget and tool-call faults ride the tool channel. The facade now: - routes malformed args (tool_arg_validation) to max_tool_errors, - returns a new action='tool_error' for tool-call faults (unknown tool name OR malformed args), with nudge.role='tool' so callers emit the correction on the tool-result channel. Channel vs budget are now two explicit kind-sets in nudge.py (TOOL_CHANNEL_KINDS ⊃ TOOL_ERROR_KINDS); unknown-tool rides the tool channel but still drains the retry budget, matching run_inference. _TOOL_ERROR_KINDS moved from inference.py to the shared nudge module. 2. Proxy exposed max_retries but not max_tool_errors, hiding the budget exactly where malformed-arg recovery now matters. Added --max-tool-errors (default 2) threaded ProxyServer → HTTPServer → handler → ErrorTracker. Nobody depends on the middleware facade yet, so the CheckResult.action addition is free. Channel parity in run_inference unchanged (it emits role=tool for list-branch corrections regardless of nudge.role). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * steps: guard arg-match prereqs against non-dict args + ADR-016 Closes the last crash vector from the design review (hole 4): StepTracker.check_prerequisites did args.get(match_arg), which raises on a non-dict args. ResponseValidator fences this before dispatch in the runner/proxy, but a granular caller that bypasses check() could reach it directly. Treat non-dict args as unsatisfied (block, don't crash). ADR-016 records the malformed-args → tool-error-channel decision in its honest framing: a native-mode conditioning bet, not an ontology claim; prompt mode degrades to the prior retry shape; the tool-error budget coupling is deliberate but revisitable. CHANGELOG held for release time. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval data: tag generations + add 32GB tier (v0.7.4) Inject a per-row `gen` field so one dashboard can fold eval waves run against different code states. gen is a comparability epoch, not a release version: v0.6.0 -> gen 1 (carries the Anthropic ablation + Retired-tier models, neither re-run since), and v0.7.0 plus the new 32GB tier -> gen 2. Rename the 32GB wave to eval_results_v0.7.4.jsonl (its landing release) and keep it as a separate file beside v0.7.0 — same gen, distinct wave, so each wave keeps its own landing commit for reproducibility. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval dashboard: eval-generation badges + retired toggle report.py now accepts multiple result files and keeps the newest gen per config (dedup_latest_gen), so the board folds all generations into one view. Lagging rows (gen < newest) get a superscript badge backed by a commit/date legend; Retired-tier models are carried forward but hidden by default (--include-retired, or a sidebar checkbox in the HTML). Adds MODEL_FAMILIES entries for the 6 32GB models so they render clean family names and cross-backend keys instead of raw GGUF stems. React dashboard: Show-retired checkbox (dimmed rows + a \"retired\" pill), superscript gen badges with provenance tooltips from the data blob. Regenerated docs/results/ from the three gen-tagged datasets. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * release: 0.7.4 — version bump, CHANGELOG, registry 32GB tier Bump 0.7.3 -> 0.7.4 and add the 0.7.4 CHANGELOG entry (malformed args -> tool-error channel; 32GB eval tier + dashboard eval-generations). Move the 6 32GB models (Mistral-Small-3.2, Qwen3.5/3.6 27-35B, Nemotron-3 Nano) from Unpublished to Current now that they're in the published eval, and reword the tier definitions for the dashboard's eval generations. This meant \"caller passed no sampling kwargs\" silently translated to \"temperature=0.7 on the wire,\" overriding backend defaults and any per-model recommendation not splatted in. Change matches the existing pattern for top_p/top_k/min_p/repeat_penalty/ presence_penalty: temperature is now optional (`float | None = None`); when None, no temperature field is sent and the backend's own default applies. - ollama.py: _build_options() initializes opts={} and conditionally adds temperature when set - llamafile.py: _apply_sampling() now also handles temperature; removed the literal \"temperature\": self.temperature from all three send-site bodies (send/send_stream/prompt-mode); _apply_sampling is already invoked at all three call sites Test updates: - Two existing tests asserted temperature in the outbound body for a default-constructed client; updated to assert absence (matches new default behavior). - Added TestTemperatureOptional in both client test files: verifies default constructor sends no temperature, and explicit temperature kwarg lands in the body. Behavior change: callers who relied on the implicit T=0.7 must now pass it explicitly. Will be noted in the v0.6.0 CHANGELOG. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Issue B: first-class recommended_sampling opt-in for clients Closes #59 follow-up: introduce a constructor flag for \"use the per-card sampling profile for this model\" that is explicit and fails loud on unknown models. API changes ----------- - New `UnsupportedModelError(ForgeError)` in `forge.errors`. Raised when `recommended_sampling=True` is passed for a model not in `MODEL_SAMPLING_DEFAULTS`. - `forge.clients.sampling_defaults` split into two functions: * `get_sampling_defaults(model)`: pure lookup. Returns the map value (a fresh copy) or `{}`. No logging, no raising. * `apply_sampling_defaults(model, *, strict)`: policy layer. | strict | model in map | behavior | |--------|--------------|-----------------------------------------| | True | yes | return dict copy | | True | no | raise UnsupportedModelError | | False | yes | one-shot INFO log; return {} | | False | no | return {} (silent) | The INFO log surfaces \"forge has opinions about this model\" without spamming on every constructor call. Fires once per (model, process). - `OllamaClient`, `LlamafileClient`, `AnthropicClient` accept `recommended_sampling: bool = False`. Caller's explicit non-None sampling kwargs win over the map field-by-field. - `AnthropicClient` accepts the flag for symmetry but it's a no-op (Anthropic SDK manages sampling internally; debug log if True passed). Removed: `get_sampling_defaults` no longer logs a warning on unknown models. Behavior moved to `apply_sampling_defaults(model, strict=False)` where it's an INFO log on supported-but-not-opted-in. Migrations ---------- - `tests/eval/batch_eval.py` and `tests/eval/eval_runner.py`: replaced `**get_sampling_defaults(...)` splat with `recommended_sampling=True`. Same outcome; unknown models in the eval list will now raise `UnsupportedModelError` instead of running silently with backend defaults. Tests ----- - `test_sampling_defaults.py`: 6 new tests covering both functions and the four-quadrant policy matrix; old `test_unknown_model_logs_once` removed (no longer applicable). - `test_ollama_client.py` / `test_llamafile_client.py`: 4 new tests each covering strict/known/unknown matrix and explicit-kwargs-win merging. - Full unit suite (857 tests) green. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Issue A: proxy passes inbound sampling fields through to backend The proxy is meant to make local forge look like an OpenAI-compatible endpoint; OpenAI honors body sampling, so forge proxy should too. Previously the proxy extracted only messages/tools/stream/model from the request body and silently discarded inbound temperature/top_p/etc. Protocol -------- - `LLMClient.send` and `LLMClient.send_stream` gain `sampling: dict[str, Any] | None = None`. Per-call overrides win over instance state field-by-field for that call only; the client's instance fields are not mutated. - Plumbed through `run_inference` and `_send_streaming` in `forge.core.inference`. Clients ------- - OllamaClient and LlamafileClient: refactored `_build_options` / `_apply_sampling` to take an optional sampling override dict and merge it over instance state at body-construction time. Recognized fields: temperature, top_p, top_k, min_p, repeat_penalty, presence_penalty, seed (seed is per-call-only — not an instance field). - AnthropicClient: accepts the kwarg for protocol symmetry but ignores it (Anthropic SDK manages sampling internally; debug log if any override is passed). Proxy ----- - `forge.proxy.handler`: new `_extract_sampling(body)` helper pulls the seven recognized OpenAI-compatible sampling fields out of the inbound body. Plumbed to `client.send()` on the no-tools path and to `run_inference()` on the tools path. The proxy's pre-built client stays a \"blank slate\"; body fields are the only sampling source in proxy mode. Docs ---- - `sampling_defaults.py` docstring: rewrote the proxy paragraph. - `docs/MODEL_GUIDE.md`: rewrote the \"How forge handles it\" section to document the new `recommended_sampling=True` flag (instead of the splat pattern), explain caller-overrides-map merge, and clarify proxy-mode pass-through behavior. - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`: replaced the splat example with the flag, added a Proxy Mode subsection with a curl example showing inbound sampling fields. Migrations ---------- - `tests/eval/eval_runner.py:CountingClientWrapper`: thread `sampling` kwarg through send / send_stream so the wrapper doesn't drop overrides. - 4 in-tree test mocks updated to accept `sampling=None` parameter (test_runner, test_slot_worker, test_eval_budget, test_context_thresholds). Tests ----- - `test_proxy_handler.py`: 4 new tests — sampling reaches client.send on no-tools path, no-fields-in-body produces sampling=None, sampling reaches run_inference on tools path, per-call overrides do not mutate client instance state across requests. - `test_ollama_client.py` / `test_llamafile_client.py`: 2 new tests each — per-call overrides win over instance, sampling=None lets instance state apply unchanged, instance fields stay unmutated. - Full unit suite (865 tests) green. Pydantic rejected at ToolCall construction, crashing the stage with ValidationError. Observed at 86% of error rows on Qwen3-Next prompt mode (77/89), same family on Qwen3.6 (rig-02). This is conceptually \"tool called with bad args\" — the call exists, the inputs are wrong — same as FileNotFoundError at runtime. Should ride the tool-error channel with max_tool_errors=2 budget, not crash. - ToolCall / TextResponse: BaseModel → @dataclass. args is no longer validated at construction; ResponseValidator enforces dict-shape. Audit: no .model_* API on ToolCall anywhere in forge. - ResponseValidator: new args-shape branch after unknown-tool check. Unknown-tool runs first (cheap; no point validating args on a hallucinated tool name). - nudges.tool_arg_validation_nudge: schema-derived message naming the tool, the received args type, and the required JSON-object shape. - inference: parse-error nudges drain max_tool_errors (record_result) not max_retries (record_retry). Message prefix [ToolArgValidationError] vs [UnknownTool]. - Exhaustion message simplified: includes which budget and nudge kind. Smoke (Ministral-3-14B-Reasoning, 26 scenarios × 25 runs prompt-mode): score 78.77% (vs v0.7.0 baseline 79.5% at n=50). Delta within ±1.6% noise band — no regression. Patch never tripped on this model; this is a regression check, full bake on a model that hits the path to follow. 884 tests pass. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * batch_eval/sampling: 32GB-eval lineup — Qwen3.6-27B, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD, Nemotron-3-Nano GGUF + server-flag + sampling-default entries for the three rig-02 32GB-tier models added for the v0.7.1 run. Launch config of record for eval_results_rig-02_v0.7.1.jsonl (31,200 rows). * clients: route malformed tool-call args through the tool-error channel Unify all OpenAI-shape clients on one decode_tool_args helper (clients/base.py): JSON-string args are parsed; malformed or non-dict payloads ride through on the ToolCall as raw (non-dict) args instead of collapsing to a TextResponse (openai_compat, vllm, llamafile) or raising (anthropic streaming). ResponseValidator's args-shape check then routes them to the tool-error channel + max_tool_errors budget — the same lane as a runtime tool error — rather than a retry nudge. Completes the client normalization #86 started (one decoder, all clients) and keeps fail-loud (never coerced to {}). Also closes an unguarded json.loads crash in the anthropic streaming finalize. Behavior change: structural malformed-args now drains max_tool_errors (2), not max_retries (3), in proxy mode. Wire-invisible; no public signature changes. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * review fixes: honest ToolCall.args type + de-stale comments + llamafile malformed test From the helper-vs-inline structural review (verdict: keep the shared decode_tool_args helper). Two correctness/honesty caveats actioned: - ToolCall.args annotated dict[str, Any] while the runtime contract now intentionally allows non-dicts (the docstring already says so). Widen to Any so the type stops lying. - Stale comments in openai_compat/vllm streaming finalize still claimed malformed args yield a retry-driving TextResponse; corrected to the raw-args → tool-error-channel routing (they invited the exact drift the helper prevents). - Add an explicit llamafile malformed-args test (non-stream native path). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * guardrails: make tool-call faults consistent across all three modes + expose proxy max_tool_errors From the H1/H2 design review. Two consistency gaps closed: 1. The Guardrails middleware facade recorded EVERY validator failure as a retry (max_retries) and returned action='retry' — diverging from run_inference/proxy, where malformed args drain the tool-error budget and tool-call faults ride the tool channel. The facade now: - routes malformed args (tool_arg_validation) to max_tool_errors, - returns a new action='tool_error' for tool-call faults (unknown tool name OR malformed args), with nudge.role='tool' so callers emit the correction on the tool-result channel. Channel vs budget are now two explicit kind-sets in nudge.py (TOOL_CHANNEL_KINDS ⊃ TOOL_ERROR_KINDS); unknown-tool rides the tool channel but still drains the retry budget, matching run_inference. _TOOL_ERROR_KINDS moved from inference.py to the shared nudge module. 2. Proxy exposed max_retries but not max_tool_errors, hiding the budget exactly where malformed-arg recovery now matters. Added --max-tool-errors (default 2) threaded ProxyServer → HTTPServer → handler → ErrorTracker. Nobody depends on the middleware facade yet, so the CheckResult.action addition is free. Channel parity in run_inference unchanged (it emits role=tool for list-branch corrections regardless of nudge.role). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * steps: guard arg-match prereqs against non-dict args + ADR-016 Closes the last crash vector from the design review (hole 4): StepTracker.check_prerequisites did args.get(match_arg), which raises on a non-dict args. ResponseValidator fences this before dispatch in the runner/proxy, but a granular caller that bypasses check() could reach it directly. Treat non-dict args as unsatisfied (block, don't crash). ADR-016 records the malformed-args → tool-error-channel decision in its honest framing: a native-mode conditioning bet, not an ontology claim; prompt mode degrades to the prior retry shape; the tool-error budget coupling is deliberate but revisitable. CHANGELOG held for release time. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval data: tag generations + add 32GB tier (v0.7.4) Inject a per-row `gen` field so one dashboard can fold eval waves run against different code states. gen is a comparability epoch, not a release version: v0.6.0 -> gen 1 (carries the Anthropic ablation + Retired-tier models, neither re-run since), and v0.7.0 plus the new 32GB tier -> gen 2. Rename the 32GB wave to eval_results_v0.7.4.jsonl (its landing release) and keep it as a separate file beside v0.7.0 — same gen, distinct wave, so each wave keeps its own landing commit for reproducibility. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval dashboard: eval-generation badges + retired toggle report.py now accepts multiple result files and keeps the newest gen per config (dedup_latest_gen), so the board folds all generations into one view. Lagging rows (gen < newest) get a superscript badge backed by a commit/date legend; Retired-tier models are carried forward but hidden by default (--include-retired, or a sidebar checkbox in the HTML). Adds MODEL_FAMILIES entries for the 6 32GB models so they render clean family names and cross-backend keys instead of raw GGUF stems. React dashboard: Show-retired checkbox (dimmed rows + a \"retired\" pill), superscript gen badges with provenance tooltips from the data blob. Regenerated docs/results/ from the three gen-tagged datasets. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * release: 0.7.4 — version bump, CHANGELOG, registry 32GB tier Bump 0.7.3 -> 0.7.4 and add the 0.7.4 CHANGELOG entry (malformed args -> tool-error channel; 32GB eval tier + dashboard eval-generations). Move the 6 32GB models (Mistral-Small-3.2, Qwen3.5/3.6 27-35B, Nemotron-3 Nano) from Unpublished to Current now that they're in the published eval, and reword the tier definitions for the dashboard's eval generations. Also scrub a stale bring-up note from the Qwen3.5-122B footnote (it leaked operator smoke-probe process into a public doc) and exclude the built dashboard dist/ from the hatchling sdist sweep. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * changelog: de-escalate dataclass entry from BREAKING to Changed The ToolCall/TextResponse pydantic->dataclass move only breaks callers who serialized these via the pydantic .model_* API or relied on construction-time validation; attribute reads and keyword construction are unchanged. Reserving BREAKING for forced-migration changes (cf. 0.7.3 --mode rename) keeps the badge meaningful. --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) ") [#102](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/pull/102) [)](https://github.com/antoinezambelli/forge/commit/bd99f4df0a7aab2fd4db2e6dae7f810a32617d76 "v0.7.4: malformed-args → tool-error channel + 32GB eval tier (#102) * Args-validation: route malformed args through tool-error channel Models occasionally emit a structurally valid tool call with malformed args content (e.g. arguments=\"\" instead of arguments=\"{}\"). Pydantic rejected at ToolCall construction, crashing the stage with ValidationError. Observed at 86% of error rows on Qwen3-Next prompt mode (77/89), same family on Qwen3.6 (rig-02). This is conceptually \"tool called with bad args\" — the call exists, the inputs are wrong — same as FileNotFoundError at runtime. Should ride the tool-error channel with max_tool_errors=2 budget, not crash. - ToolCall / TextResponse: BaseModel → @dataclass. args is no longer validated at construction; ResponseValidator enforces dict-shape. Audit: no .model_* API on ToolCall anywhere in forge. - ResponseValidator: new args-shape branch after unknown-tool check. Unknown-tool runs first (cheap; no point validating args on a hallucinated tool name). - nudges.tool_arg_validation_nudge: schema-derived message naming the tool, the received args type, and the required JSON-object shape. - inference: parse-error nudges drain max_tool_errors (record_result) not max_retries (record_retry). Message prefix [ToolArgValidationError] vs [UnknownTool]. - Exhaustion message simplified: includes which budget and nudge kind. Smoke (Ministral-3-14B-Reasoning, 26 scenarios × 25 runs prompt-mode): score 78.77% (vs v0.7.0 baseline 79.5% at n=50). Delta within ±1.6% noise band — no regression. Patch never tripped on this model; this is a regression check, full bake on a model that hits the path to follow. 884 tests pass. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * batch_eval/sampling: 32GB-eval lineup — Qwen3.6-27B, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD, Nemotron-3-Nano GGUF + server-flag + sampling-default entries for the three rig-02 32GB-tier models added for the v0.7.1 run. Launch config of record for eval_results_rig-02_v0.7.1.jsonl (31,200 rows). * clients: route malformed tool-call args through the tool-error channel Unify all OpenAI-shape clients on one decode_tool_args helper (clients/base.py): JSON-string args are parsed; malformed or non-dict payloads ride through on the ToolCall as raw (non-dict) args instead of collapsing to a TextResponse (openai_compat, vllm, llamafile) or raising (anthropic streaming). ResponseValidator's args-shape check then routes them to the tool-error channel + max_tool_errors budget — the same lane as a runtime tool error — rather than a retry nudge. Completes the client normalization #86 started (one decoder, all clients) and keeps fail-loud (never coerced to {}). Also closes an unguarded json.loads crash in the anthropic streaming finalize. Behavior change: structural malformed-args now drains max_tool_errors (2), not max_retries (3), in proxy mode. Wire-invisible; no public signature changes. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * review fixes: honest ToolCall.args type + de-stale comments + llamafile malformed test From the helper-vs-inline structural review (verdict: keep the shared decode_tool_args helper). Two correctness/honesty caveats actioned: - ToolCall.args annotated dict[str, Any] while the runtime contract now intentionally allows non-dicts (the docstring already says so). Widen to Any so the type stops lying. - Stale comments in openai_compat/vllm streaming finalize still claimed malformed args yield a retry-driving TextResponse; corrected to the raw-args → tool-error-channel routing (they invited the exact drift the helper prevents). - Add an explicit llamafile malformed-args test (non-stream native path). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * guardrails: make tool-call faults consistent across all three modes + expose proxy max_tool_errors From the H1/H2 design review. Two consistency gaps closed: 1. The Guardrails middleware facade recorded EVERY validator failure as a retry (max_retries) and returned action='retry' — diverging from run_inference/proxy, where malformed args drain the tool-error budget and tool-call faults ride the tool channel. The facade now: - routes malformed args (tool_arg_validation) to max_tool_errors, - returns a new action='tool_error' for tool-call faults (unknown tool name OR malformed args), with nudge.role='tool' so callers emit the correction on the tool-result channel. Channel vs budget are now two explicit kind-sets in nudge.py (TOOL_CHANNEL_KINDS ⊃ TOOL_ERROR_KINDS); unknown-tool rides the tool channel but still drains the retry budget, matching run_inference. _TOOL_ERROR_KINDS moved from inference.py to the shared nudge module. 2. Proxy exposed max_retries but not max_tool_errors, hiding the budget exactly where malformed-arg recovery now matters. Added --max-tool-errors (default 2) threaded ProxyServer → HTTPServer → handler → ErrorTracker. Nobody depends on the middleware facade yet, so the CheckResult.action addition is free. Channel parity in run_inference unchanged (it emits role=tool for list-branch corrections regardless of nudge.role). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * steps: guard arg-match prereqs against non-dict args + ADR-016 Closes the last crash vector from the design review (hole 4): StepTracker.check_prerequisites did args.get(match_arg), which raises on a non-dict args. Pydantic rejected at ToolCall construction, crashing the stage with ValidationError. Observed at 86% of error rows on Qwen3-Next prompt mode (77/89), same family on Qwen3.6 (rig-02). This is conceptually \"tool called with bad args\" — the call exists, the inputs are wrong — same as FileNotFoundError at runtime. Should ride the tool-error channel with max_tool_errors=2 budget, not crash. - ToolCall / TextResponse: BaseModel → @dataclass. args is no longer validated at construction; ResponseValidator enforces dict-shape. Audit: no .model_* API on ToolCall anywhere in forge. - ResponseValidator: new args-shape branch after unknown-tool check. Unknown-tool runs first (cheap; no point validating args on a hallucinated tool name). - nudges.tool_arg_validation_nudge: schema-derived message naming the tool, the received args type, and the required JSON-object shape. - inference: parse-error nudges drain max_tool_errors (record_result) not max_retries (record_retry). Message prefix [ToolArgValidationError] vs [UnknownTool]. - Exhaustion message simplified: includes which budget and nudge kind. Smoke (Ministral-3-14B-Reasoning, 26 scenarios × 25 runs prompt-mode): score 78.77% (vs v0.7.0 baseline 79.5% at n=50). Delta within ±1.6% noise band — no regression. Patch never tripped on this model; this is a regression check, full bake on a model that hits the path to follow. 884 tests pass. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * batch_eval/sampling: 32GB-eval lineup — Qwen3.6-27B, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD, Nemotron-3-Nano GGUF + server-flag + sampling-default entries for the three rig-02 32GB-tier models added for the v0.7.1 run. Launch config of record for eval_results_rig-02_v0.7.1.jsonl (31,200 rows). * clients: route malformed tool-call args through the tool-error channel Unify all OpenAI-shape clients on one decode_tool_args helper (clients/base.py): JSON-string args are parsed; malformed or non-dict payloads ride through on the ToolCall as raw (non-dict) args instead of collapsing to a TextResponse (openai_compat, vllm, llamafile) or raising (anthropic streaming). ResponseValidator's args-shape check then routes them to the tool-error channel + max_tool_errors budget — the same lane as a runtime tool error — rather than a retry nudge. Completes the client normalization #86 started (one decoder, all clients) and keeps fail-loud (never coerced to {}). Also closes an unguarded json.loads crash in the anthropic streaming finalize. Behavior change: structural malformed-args now drains max_tool_errors (2), not max_retries (3), in proxy mode. Wire-invisible; no public signature changes. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * review fixes: honest ToolCall.args type + de-stale comments + llamafile malformed test From the helper-vs-inline structural review (verdict: keep the shared decode_tool_args helper). Two correctness/honesty caveats actioned: - ToolCall.args annotated dict[str, Any] while the runtime contract now intentionally allows non-dicts (the docstring already says so). Widen to Any so the type stops lying. - Stale comments in openai_compat/vllm streaming finalize still claimed malformed args yield a retry-driving TextResponse; corrected to the raw-args → tool-error-channel routing (they invited the exact drift the helper prevents). - Add an explicit llamafile malformed-args test (non-stream native path). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * guardrails: make tool-call faults consistent across all three modes + expose proxy max_tool_errors From the H1/H2 design review. Two consistency gaps closed: 1. The Guardrails middleware facade recorded EVERY validator failure as a retry (max_retries) and returned action='retry' — diverging from run_inference/proxy, where malformed args drain the tool-error budget and tool-call faults ride the tool channel. The facade now: - routes malformed args (tool_arg_validation) to max_tool_errors, - returns a new action='tool_error' for tool-call faults (unknown tool name OR malformed args), with nudge.role='tool' so callers emit the correction on the tool-result channel. Channel vs budget are now two explicit kind-sets in nudge.py (TOOL_CHANNEL_KINDS ⊃ TOOL_ERROR_KINDS); unknown-tool rides the tool channel but still drains the retry budget, matching run_inference. _TOOL_ERROR_KINDS moved from inference.py to the shared nudge module. 2. Proxy exposed max_retries but not max_tool_errors, hiding the budget exactly where malformed-arg recovery now matters. Added --max-tool-errors (default 2) threaded ProxyServer → HTTPServer → handler → ErrorTracker. Nobody depends on the middleware facade yet, so the CheckResult.action addition is free. Channel parity in run_inference unchanged (it emits role=tool for list-branch corrections regardless of nudge.role). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * steps: guard arg-match prereqs against non-dict args + ADR-016 Closes the last crash vector from the design review (hole 4): StepTracker.check_prerequisites did args.get(match_arg), which raises on a non-dict args. ResponseValidator fences this before dispatch in the runner/proxy, but a granular caller that bypasses check() could reach it directly. Treat non-dict args as unsatisfied (block, don't crash). ADR-016 records the malformed-args → tool-error-channel decision in its honest framing: a native-mode conditioning bet, not an ontology claim; prompt mode degrades to the prior retry shape; the tool-error budget coupling is deliberate but revisitable. CHANGELOG held for release time. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval data: tag generations + add 32GB tier (v0.7.4) Inject a per-row `gen` field so one dashboard can fold eval waves run against different code states. gen is a comparability epoch, not a release version: v0.6.0 -> gen 1 (carries the Anthropic ablation + Retired-tier models, neither re-run since), and v0.7.0 plus the new 32GB tier -> gen 2. Rename the 32GB wave to eval_results_v0.7.4.jsonl (its landing release) and keep it as a separate file beside v0.7.0 — same gen, distinct wave, so each wave keeps its own landing commit for reproducibility. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval dashboard: eval-generation badges + retired toggle report.py now accepts multiple result files and keeps the newest gen per config (dedup_latest_gen), so the board folds all generations into one view. Lagging rows (gen < newest) get a superscript badge backed by a commit/date legend; Retired-tier models are carried forward but hidden by default (--include-retired, or a sidebar checkbox in the HTML). Adds MODEL_FAMILIES entries for the 6 32GB models so they render clean family names and cross-backend keys instead of raw GGUF stems. React dashboard: Show-retired checkbox (dimmed rows + a \"retired\" pill), superscript gen badges with provenance tooltips from the data blob. Regenerated docs/results/ from the three gen-tagged datasets. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * release: 0.7.4 — version bump, CHANGELOG, registry 32GB tier Bump 0.7.3 -> 0.7.4 and add the 0.7.4 CHANGELOG entry (malformed args -> tool-error channel; 32GB eval tier + dashboard eval-generations). Move the 6 32GB models (Mistral-Small-3.2, Qwen3.5/3.6 27-35B, Nemotron-3 Nano) from Unpublished to Current now that they're in the published eval, and reword the tier definitions for the dashboard's eval generations. Also scrub a stale bring-up note from the Qwen3.5-122B footnote (it leaked operator smoke-probe process into a public doc) and exclude the built dashboard dist/ from the hatchling sdist sweep. Thread top_p/top_k/min_p/repeat_penalty/presence_penalty through OllamaClient and LlamafileClient as optional kwargs (None = don't send, backend default applies). Add forge.clients.sampling_defaults with a per-model map sourced one card at a time from HuggingFace, with each row carrying an inline URL comment pointing at the card it was pulled from. 23 verified rows across Qwen3/3.5/3.6, Qwen3-Coder, Gemma 4, Mistral Small 3.2, Devstral Small 2, Ministral 3 Instruct + Reasoning, and Mistral Nemo. Consumers in managed mode (batch_eval.py, eval_runner.py) now splat get_sampling_defaults(model) into client construction. Proxy stays as a pure passthrough — no map consultation. AnthropicClient no longer sends a hardcoded temperature; the API's own defaults apply, since Claude is frontier-optimized and forge's use of it is a baseline-comparison tool. Documented in MODEL_GUIDE.md with a human-readable table of supported models and source links, plus a shorter cross-reference in USER_GUIDE.md. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Rebuild ablation runner for rig-00 re-run: Qwen + llamafile, reforged+bare Drops the 12GB-tier + Granite plan. New plan is all Qwen3 variants across all backends plus all llamafile configs — reforged+bare only, driven by the model-params sampling-defaults change. Adds --output flag passed through to batch_eval so rig-00 data lands in eval_results_rig-00.jsonl, keeping the point-in-time eval_results file intact. 28 batches total, ~78h estimated. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Ignore scripts/run_ablation.bat — rig-specific wrapper The .bat wrapper carries rig-specific absolute paths (python, models-dir) and belongs only on the rig that runs the Scheduled Task. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Ignore eval_results_rig*.jsonl rig-local result files Per-rig eval result files live only on the rig that produced them (eval_results_rig-00.jsonl, etc.). Ignoring keeps them out of git status clutter and prevents accidental staging via 'git add .'. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Add Qwen3-4B-2507 instruct + thinking sampling defaults Two new rows extending the per-model sampling map from 1588c14: - qwen3:4b-instruct-2507-q4_K_M (temp 0.7, top_p 0.8, top_k 20) - qwen3:4b-thinking-2507-q4_K_M (temp 0.6, top_p 0.95, top_k 20) Values verified against the official HF model cards (URLs in inline comments). MODEL_GUIDE.md table updated to match. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Add data_gap_recovery_extended scenario (lambda + stateful) Harder hint-chain variant designed to weed out top-tier models after the sampling-params fix lifted 8B-class to 100% on the existing suite. - 12 tools (vs DGR's 7), 5 facts (vs 3), 2-3 hop chains - No direct tool-name hints in get_employee — model must discover downstream tools by name and infer routing from domain language - security_audit returns provisioned access groups that look like a complete answer (soft trap — missing -v3/-v2 suffixes the validator wants); model must find project_assignments to get current versions - personnel_file uses domain language only (\"current pay system\", \"HR onboarding\") — no compensation_v2 or onboarding_records names - 2 misleading-by-name traps (compensation_history, access_control) - 1 status-marker lure (legacy_records — plausible-but-stale data) - Indirect validation via downstream-only-content (B7, payments-prod-v3, internal-apis-v2 only appear in correct downstream tool returns) Surface CPU N=10 smoke on qwen3:4b-instruct-2507-q4_K_M: baseline DGR 100% / DGE 80% correctness — 20pt drop, hardening landed. 40 unit tests cover tool callables, validator (canonical + each failure mode), scenario shape, stateful backend, registration. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Add advanced_reasoning scenario module: DGE refactor + ART - Split data_gap_recovery_extended (lambda + stateful) out of _model_quality into new _model_reasoning / _stateful_model_reasoning modules tagged 'advanced_reasoning' for top-tier-only batching. - Add argument_transformation (lambda + stateful): Q4 2024 expense audit with EUR->USD conversion, vendor case-mismatch disambiguation, and boundary date filtering. Designed as a top-tier separator alongside DGE. - Update __init__ re-exports and DGE test imports for new module layout. * Add inconsistent_api_recovery scenario (lambda + stateful) Cascading error recovery across seven heterogeneously-designed legacy APIs. Each tool violates a different convention dimension (ID format, date format, units, casing, separator, encoding) so the recovery lesson from one tool is wrong for the next. Tool descriptions are minimal and the system prompt is terse — recovery hints live only in the error messages, matching the existing error_recovery pattern. Designed as a guardrails-vs-bare ablation scenario and a floor separator: 4B completes ~90% but fails ~100% of the time on the math-on-top-of-format-recovery layer; Haiku saturates at 100/100 across both variants. Complements DGE/ART which target top-tier spread. * Add grounded_synthesis scenario (lambda + stateful) Synthesis under attractor pressure — model receives pre-distilled sub-agent assessments via tools and must produce a hiring decision that respects implicit constraints surfaced narratively across the returns. Mirrors the failure mode observed in 40kAIo's Commander, where small models recommended actions that violated keyword exclusions buried in tier-2 summaries. Pool: 5 candidates, 3 with blockers and 2 viable: - Sarah: attractor (top-decile payments) + non-compete waiver process - James: attractor (top-decile distributed-systems) + visa sponsorship gap - Diana: hidden-blocker attractor — clean compatibility, but team-dynamics reveals role mismatch (lead/EM track vs IC) and short-tenure / low-commitment signal - Aisha: eligible, payments-experienced, fast start (CORRECT pick) - Marcus: eligible, weaker preference (less experience, no payments) Tool returns use soft qualitative language only — no explicit timeline numbers, eligibility headers, or probabilities. Model must infer that \"drawn-out, no guarantee\" / \"slow process, mixed success\" / \"fall through, keep an eye out\" do not fit the role's \"Q3 product launch\" timing context. Validator accepts a correct pick (Aisha) plus either a blocker synonym in the rationale OR a named rejection of one of the unsuccessful candidates. Empirical (4B / Haiku N=20 each): - 4B 0/20 lambda + 0/20 stateful — confidently wrong - Haiku 20/20 lambda + 19/20 stateful — connects soft signals reliably * Track rig-local eval JSONLs via LFS, add rig-01 results Reverses the eval_results_rig*.jsonl gitignore so per-rig results stay in the repo. Adds an LFS attribute pattern for rig*.jsonl matching the existing eval_results.jsonl rule. eval_results_rig-01.jsonl: 60 configs × 900 runs = 54000 rows covering the non-Qwen, non-llamafile slate (llama3.1, mistral 7b v0.3, mistral-nemo, ministral-3 instruct + reasoning) on this rig's available backends (ollama + llamaserver native + llamaserver prompt). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Add rig-00 ablation results: 25 batches, 22500 rows Covers the v0.6.0 ablation slate that ran on rig-00 after splitting work with rig-02 (which took 3 qwen3-14b-q4 llamaserver batches): - All llamafile configs (5 models × {reforged, bare}): llama3.1-8b-q4/q8, mistral-nemo-12b, mistral-7b-v0.3-q4/q8 - All Qwen3 ollama configs (3 models × {reforged, bare}): qwen3-8b-q4/q8, qwen3-14b-q4 - All Qwen3 llamaserver configs except the 3 that ran on rig-02: qwen3-8b-q4 (native + prompt), qwen3-8b-q8 (native + prompt), qwen3-14b-q4 (native, reforged only) Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Add v0.6.0 sampling cleanup spec Working spec capturing three connected fixes before v0.6.0 finalization: proxy body pass-through, recommended_sampling opt-in flag with split get/apply functions, and dropping the hardcoded temperature=0.7 default from OllamaClient and LlamafileClient. Single PR plan inside the doc. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * rig-01 results: 60 configs × 26 scenarios × 50 runs (advanced_reasoning + OG-18) Adds the 8 new advanced_reasoning scenarios (data_gap_recovery_extended, argument_transformation, inconsistent_api_recovery, grounded_synthesis × lambda+stateful) on top of the existing OG-18 model_quality run. Backfills 5 LS/native/reforged batches that hit transient server-startup gaps in the first run. Final: 78000 rows, all 60 configs at full coverage. Partial qwen3:14b ollama reforged rows (reallocated from rig-00) purged — the model averaged 30s/run with two new scenarios spiraling >70s, deemed not viable on this hardware for the new suite. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * rig-02 results: qwen3 advanced_reasoning sweep + qwen3:14b OG-18 offload (7500 rows) Adds the 8 advanced_reasoning scenarios (lambda + stateful x4) on dual 5070 Tis for the three qwen3 model configs assigned to rig-02: - qwen3:8b-q4 LS/native + LS/prompt (bare + reforged) - qwen3:8b-q8 LS/native + LS/prompt (bare + reforged) - qwen3:14b-q4 LS/native + LS/prompt (bare + reforged) 4800 AR rows total, split across two GPUs concurrently via per-GPU llama-server ports (8080/8081) + CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES pinning. Also includes the 3 qwen3:14b-q4 LS OG-18 cells (native/bare, prompt/bare, prompt/reforged) that were offloaded from rig-01 — 2700 rows. These cells are deliberately absent from rig-01's audit set. qwen3:8b ollama AR cells skipped — model averaged ~230s/run with scores at 1% accuracy on the new suite, deemed not viable on this hardware. Documented as a known gap for v0.6.0; community evals welcome. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Update rig-00 results: drop incomplete Qwen-ollama configs, add llamafile advanced_reasoning rig-00 file went from 22500 -> 21100 rows (net -1400): - Added: 4000 new advanced_reasoning rows from 5 llamafile configs (llama3.1-8b-q4/q8, mistral-nemo-12b, mistral-7b-q4/q8) x {reforged, bare} - Removed: 5400 OG-18 rows from 3 Qwen-ollama configs (qwen3-8b-q4, qwen3-8b-q8, qwen3-14b-q4) x {reforged, bare} Qwen-ollama is dropped from the v0.6.0 ablation matrix because the backend x suite combination was not viable on advanced_reasoning runtime (timeout-class slowness on both 5070 and 5070 Ti rigs). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Add data_gap_recovery_extended scenario (lambda + stateful) Harder hint-chain variant designed to weed out top-tier models after the sampling-params fix lifted 8B-class to 100% on the existing suite. - 12 tools (vs DGR's 7), 5 facts (vs 3), 2-3 hop chains - No direct tool-name hints in get_employee — model must discover downstream tools by name and infer routing from domain language - security_audit returns provisioned access groups that look like a complete answer (soft trap — missing -v3/-v2 suffixes the validator wants); model must find project_assignments to get current versions - personnel_file uses domain language only (\"current pay system\", \"HR onboarding\") — no compensation_v2 or onboarding_records names - 2 misleading-by-name traps (compensation_history, access_control) - 1 status-marker lure (legacy_records — plausible-but-stale data) - Indirect validation via downstream-only-content (B7, payments-prod-v3, internal-apis-v2 only appear in correct downstream tool returns) Surface CPU N=10 smoke on qwen3:4b-instruct-2507-q4_K_M: baseline DGR 100% / DGE 80% correctness — 20pt drop, hardening landed. 40 unit tests cover tool callables, validator (canonical + each failure mode), scenario shape, stateful backend, registration. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * Add advanced_reasoning scenario module: DGE refactor + ART - Split data_gap_recovery_extended (lambda + stateful) out of _model_quality into new _model_reasoning / _stateful_model_reasoning modules tagged 'advanced_reasoning' for top-tier-only batching. - Add argument_transformation (lambda + stateful): Q4 2024 expense audit with EUR->USD conversion, vendor case-mismatch disambiguation, and boundary date filtering. Designed as a top-tier separator alongside DGE. - Update __init__ re-exports and DGE test imports for new module layout. * Add inconsistent_api_recovery scenario (lambda + stateful) Cascading error recovery across seven heterogeneously-designed legacy APIs. Each tool violates a different convention dimension (ID format, date format, units, casing, separator, encoding) so the recovery lesson from one tool is wrong for the next. Tool descriptions are minimal and the system prompt is terse — recovery hints live only in the error messages, matching the existing error_recovery pattern. Designed as a guardrails-vs-bare ablation scenario and a floor separator: 4B completes ~90% but fails ~100% of the time on the math-on-top-of-format-recovery layer; Haiku saturates at 100/100 across both variants. Complements DGE/ART which target top-tier spread. * Add grounded_synthesis scenario (lambda + stateful) Synthesis under attractor pressure — model receives pre-distilled sub-agent assessments via tools and must produce a hiring decision that respects implicit constraints surfaced narratively across the returns. Mirrors the failure mode observed in 40kAIo's Commander, where small models recommended actions that violated keyword exclusions buried in tier-2 summaries. Pool: 5 candidates, 3 with blockers and 2 viable: - Sarah: attractor (top-decile payments) + non-compete waiver process - James: attractor (top-decile distributed-systems) + visa sponsorship gap - Diana: hidden-blocker attractor — clean compatibility, but team-dynamics reveals role mismatch (lead/EM track vs IC) and short-tenure / low-commitment signal - Aisha: eligible, payments-experienced, fast start (CORRECT pick) - Marcus: eligible, weaker preference (less experience, no payments) Tool returns use soft qualitative language only — no explicit timeline numbers, eligibility headers, or probabilities. Model must infer that \"drawn-out, no guarantee\" / \"slow process, mixed success\" / \"fall through, keep an eye out\" do not fit the role's \"Q3 product launch\" timing context. Validator accepts a correct pick (Aisha) plus either a blocker synonym in the rationale OR a named rejection of one of the unsuccessful candidates. Pydantic rejected at ToolCall construction, crashing the stage with ValidationError. Observed at 86% of error rows on Qwen3-Next prompt mode (77/89), same family on Qwen3.6 (rig-02). This is conceptually \"tool called with bad args\" — the call exists, the inputs are wrong — same as FileNotFoundError at runtime. Should ride the tool-error channel with max_tool_errors=2 budget, not crash. - ToolCall / TextResponse: BaseModel → @dataclass. args is no longer validated at construction; ResponseValidator enforces dict-shape. Audit: no .model_* API on ToolCall anywhere in forge. - ResponseValidator: new args-shape branch after unknown-tool check. Unknown-tool runs first (cheap; no point validating args on a hallucinated tool name). - nudges.tool_arg_validation_nudge: schema-derived message naming the tool, the received args type, and the required JSON-object shape. - inference: parse-error nudges drain max_tool_errors (record_result) not max_retries (record_retry). Message prefix [ToolArgValidationError] vs [UnknownTool]. - Exhaustion message simplified: includes which budget and nudge kind. Smoke (Ministral-3-14B-Reasoning, 26 scenarios × 25 runs prompt-mode): score 78.77% (vs v0.7.0 baseline 79.5% at n=50). Delta within ±1.6% noise band — no regression. Patch never tripped on this model; this is a regression check, full bake on a model that hits the path to follow. 884 tests pass. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) * batch_eval/sampling: 32GB-eval lineup — Qwen3.6-27B, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD, Nemotron-3-Nano GGUF + server-flag + sampling-default entries for the three rig-02 32GB-tier models added for the v0.7.1 run. Launch config of record for eval_results_rig-02_v0.7.1.jsonl (31,200 rows). * clients: route malformed tool-call args through the tool-error channel Unify all OpenAI-shape clients on one decode_tool_args helper (clients/base.py): JSON-string args are parsed; malformed or non-dict payloads ride through on the ToolCall as raw (non-dict) args instead of collapsing to a TextResponse (openai_compat, vllm, llamafile) or raising (anthropic streaming). ResponseValidator's args-shape check then routes them to the tool-error channel + max_tool_errors budget — the same lane as a runtime tool error — rather than a retry nudge. Completes the client normalization #86 started (one decoder, all clients) and keeps fail-loud (never coerced to {}). Also closes an unguarded json.loads crash in the anthropic streaming finalize. Behavior change: structural malformed-args now drains max_tool_errors (2), not max_retries (3), in proxy mode. Wire-invisible; no public signature changes. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * review fixes: honest ToolCall.args type + de-stale comments + llamafile malformed test From the helper-vs-inline structural review (verdict: keep the shared decode_tool_args helper). Two correctness/honesty caveats actioned: - ToolCall.args annotated dict[str, Any] while the runtime contract now intentionally allows non-dicts (the docstring already says so). Widen to Any so the type stops lying. - Stale comments in openai_compat/vllm streaming finalize still claimed malformed args yield a retry-driving TextResponse; corrected to the raw-args → tool-error-channel routing (they invited the exact drift the helper prevents). - Add an explicit llamafile malformed-args test (non-stream native path). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * guardrails: make tool-call faults consistent across all three modes + expose proxy max_tool_errors From the H1/H2 design review. Two consistency gaps closed: 1. The Guardrails middleware facade recorded EVERY validator failure as a retry (max_retries) and returned action='retry' — diverging from run_inference/proxy, where malformed args drain the tool-error budget and tool-call faults ride the tool channel. The facade now: - routes malformed args (tool_arg_validation) to max_tool_errors, - returns a new action='tool_error' for tool-call faults (unknown tool name OR malformed args), with nudge.role='tool' so callers emit the correction on the tool-result channel. Channel vs budget are now two explicit kind-sets in nudge.py (TOOL_CHANNEL_KINDS ⊃ TOOL_ERROR_KINDS); unknown-tool rides the tool channel but still drains the retry budget, matching run_inference. _TOOL_ERROR_KINDS moved from inference.py to the shared nudge module. 2. Proxy exposed max_retries but not max_tool_errors, hiding the budget exactly where malformed-arg recovery now matters. Added --max-tool-errors (default 2) threaded ProxyServer → HTTPServer → handler → ErrorTracker. Nobody depends on the middleware facade yet, so the CheckResult.action addition is free. Channel parity in run_inference unchanged (it emits role=tool for list-branch corrections regardless of nudge.role). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * steps: guard arg-match prereqs against non-dict args + ADR-016 Closes the last crash vector from the design review (hole 4): StepTracker.check_prerequisites did args.get(match_arg), which raises on a non-dict args. ResponseValidator fences this before dispatch in the runner/proxy, but a granular caller that bypasses check() could reach it directly. Treat non-dict args as unsatisfied (block, don't crash). ADR-016 records the malformed-args → tool-error-channel decision in its honest framing: a native-mode conditioning bet, not an ontology claim; prompt mode degrades to the prior retry shape; the tool-error budget coupling is deliberate but revisitable. CHANGELOG held for release time. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval data: tag generations + add 32GB tier (v0.7.4) Inject a per-row `gen` field so one dashboard can fold eval waves run against different code states. gen is a comparability epoch, not a release version: v0.6.0 -> gen 1 (carries the Anthropic ablation + Retired-tier models, neither re-run since), and v0.7.0 plus the new 32GB tier -> gen 2. Rename the 32GB wave to eval_results_v0.7.4.jsonl (its landing release) and keep it as a separate file beside v0.7.0 — same gen, distinct wave, so each wave keeps its own landing commit for reproducibility. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * eval dashboard: eval-generation badges + retired toggle report.py now accepts multiple result files and keeps the newest gen per config (dedup_latest_gen), so the board folds all generations into one view. Lagging rows (gen < newest) get a superscript badge backed by a commit/date legend; Retired-tier models are carried forward but hidden by default (--include-retired, or a sidebar checkbox in the HTML). Adds MODEL_FAMILIES entries for the 6 32GB models so they render clean family names and cross-backend keys instead of raw GGUF stems. React dashboard: Show-retired checkbox (dimmed rows + a \"retired\" pill), superscript gen badges with provenance tooltips from the data blob. Regenerated docs/results/ from the three gen-tagged datasets. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 * release: 0.7.4 — version bump, CHANGELOG, registry 32GB tier Bump 0.7.3 -> 0.7.4 and add the 0.7.4 CHANGELOG entry (malformed args -> tool-error channel; 32GB eval tier + dashboard eval-generations). Move the 6 32GB models (Mistral-Small-3.2, Qwen3.5/3.6 27-35B, Nemotron-3 Nano) from Unpublished to Current now that they're in the published eval, and reword the tier definitions for the dashboard's eval generations. Also scrub a stale bring-up note from the Qwen3.5-122B footnote (it leaked operator smoke-probe process into a public doc) and exclude the built dashboard dist/ from the hatchling sdist sweep. Behavior change: structural malformed-args now drains max_tool_errors (2), not max_retries (3), in proxy mode. Wire-invisible; no public signature changes. Widen to Any so the type stops lying. - Stale comments in openai_compat/vllm streaming finalize still claimed malformed args yield a retry-driving TextResponse; corrected to the raw-args → tool-error-channel routing (they invited the exact drift the helper prevents). - Add an explicit llamafile malformed-args test (non-stream native path). The facade now: - routes malformed args (tool_arg_validation) to max_tool_errors, - returns a new action='tool_error' for tool-call faults (unknown tool name OR malformed args), with nudge.role='tool' so callers emit the correction on the tool-result channel. Channel vs budget are now two explicit kind-sets in nudge.py (TOOL_CHANNEL_KINDS ⊃ TOOL_ERROR_KINDS); unknown-tool rides the tool channel but still drains the retry budget, matching run_inference. _TOOL_ERROR_KINDS moved from inference.py to the shared nudge module. 2. Channel parity in run_inference unchanged (it emits role=tool for list-branch corrections regardless of nudge.role).
